source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Question: 426
A small college is expecting that the ratio of students that will pass compared to those that will fail will be 3 to 1. The college has 880 students. Despite these expectations, 639 students passed, while 241 failed. What is the correct chi-square statistic?
extraction_notes: answer field is numeric (2); mapped to choice text "2.67" from the provided choices
image field contains a PIL.Image object description, not a path/URL
source_intake_record.subject: Math

image_dependency: question_text provides all needed quantities and ratio in text
an image is registered, but nothing in the question refers to a diagram, chart, or visual attribute
multi_step: solving requires converting the expected 3:1 ratio into expected counts out of 880
then comparing observed counts (639, 241) to expected counts and computing the chi-square sum across categories
this is more than direct lookup but still a short structured calculation
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is provided as 2.67
multiple-choice options are given, enabling easy final-answer checking
the computation is deterministic from the stated values and standard chi-square formula

decision_reasons: question, answer, and image assets are all present with no registry issues
verifiability is very high because the answer is numeric and the computation is deterministic from the provided text
the problem is recoverable and usable even without relying on the image, so the image_present_but_not_needed flag is not a core semantic risk
multi-step reasoning demand is moderate, supporting candidate value
